name: Cfg Bank
description_short: Privately-owned bank headquartered in Baltimore offering personal and commercial banking with competitive deposit rates and a focus on personalized client relationships.
description_long:
CFG Bank is a privately-owned financial institution based in Baltimore, Maryland, with a unique corporate structure that allows it to operate with fewer regulatory hurdles than traditional banks. Founded by an owner committed to community impact (evidenced by the creation of the Dwyer Workforce Development nonprofit), CFG Bank positions itself as the "un-bank" – emphasizing personalized relationships over impersonal corporate banking.

The bank offers comprehensive banking services across three main divisions: Personal Banking (checking, savings, deposit accounts), Commercial Banking (business accounts and lending), and specialized Lending (Bridge loans, HUD loans, and Agency financing). They advertise competitive deposit rates comparable to the best in the country and provide online banking platforms with tax document access, eStatements, and loan payment portals. The bank is FDIC insured with a routing number of 052073519.

CFG Bank distinguishes itself through several factors: private ownership structure, competitive rates, a strong workplace culture (ranked #1 Best Place to Work by Baltimore Business Journal for three consecutive years), and specialized lending expertise in commercial real estate (particularly skilled nursing facilities, with $3.1+ billion financed in 2025). The bank emphasizes bold decision-making, accessibility (AudioEye-enabled website), and deep client relationships supported by dedicated commercial centers and personalized service.

The main limitations are geographic: CFG Bank operates only in Maryland with three physical branches (Lutherville, Fells Point, and Annapolis – the latter being cashless-only). While they offer competitive rates and specialized commercial lending, customers seeking nationwide branch access, extensive ATM networks, or full digital-only neobanking may find traditional or online banks more convenient. Their strength lies in personalized commercial lending and local Maryland personal banking rather than national retail banking scale.
services: Personal checking and savings accounts with competitive deposit rates
Commercial business checking and deposit accounts
Online banking portal with eStatements and tax form access (1099s)
Bridge loan financing for real estate transactions
Commercial lending for business acquisitions and growth
Specialized skilled nursing facility and healthcare real estate financing
pros: FDIC insured with competitive deposit rates comparable to top national banks
Privately owned structure allows faster decision-making and personalized service without traditional corporate bureaucracy
Ranked #1 Best Place to Work for three consecutive years by Baltimore Business Journal, indicating strong company culture and employee retention
Specialized expertise in complex commercial lending ($3.1+ billion financed in 2025), including bridge loans, HUD financing, and skilled nursing facility acquisitions
Owner's commitment to community impact through Dwyer Workforce Development nonprofit addressing healthcare workforce and poverty
Online banking portal with accessible features (AudioEye enabled) and convenient tax form access for paperless-enrolled customers
Multiple service lines including personal, commercial, and specialized lending handled by dedicated teams with personalized support
cons: Limited geographic footprint – only three Maryland branches (Lutherville, Fells Point, Annapolis) with no national presence or extensive ATM network
Annapolis branch is cashless-only, limiting in-person cash transaction options
No evidence of mobile-first digital banking, cryptocurrency, or other fintech features that online banks offer
As a regional bank, may lack the account protection insurance or investment products available at larger national institutions
Contact form on website explicitly states it is not secure and warns against submitting personal information
best_for: Maryland-based small business owners and entrepreneurs seeking personalized commercial lending and specialized financing for acquisitions
Personal banking customers in Baltimore and surrounding areas who value relationship-based banking over large corporate institutions
Commercial real estate investors and operators in healthcare (skilled nursing facilities, medical practices) seeking specialized loan expertise
Businesses with complex financing needs (bridge loans, HUD loans, agency financing) that benefit from faster decision-making and personalized structures
diagnosis: CFG Bank is best for Maryland-based businesses and individuals seeking personalized, relationship-driven banking with specialized commercial lending expertise – particularly in healthcare real estate. The main caveat is geographic limitation: customers outside Maryland or those seeking national branch networks and digital-first features will find larger banks or neobanks more suitable.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
